sections.paragraph: in pause שְׂלָו (in שְׂלָיו קרי), a quail, so called from its fatness (compare Arab. سمانى), always collect. quails, Exo 16:13 (and there with a fem. in the manner of collectives), Num 11:32 Psa 105:40. Plur. שַׂלְוִים Num 11:32, from the sing. שַׂלְוֶה, comp. Arab. سَلْوَى and Sam. שלוי, which is also in the Hebræo-Sam. copy. LXX. ὀρτυγομήτρα. Vulg. coturnix. See Bochart, Hieroz. ii. p. 92, J. E. Faber on Harmer’s Observations, vol. ii, p. 441. Niebuhr’s Beschr. von Arabien, p. 176.
